title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)
quote: Google announced A2A on 2025-04-09 as an open protocol for agent interoperability, built on HTTP, SSE, and JSON-RPC with long-running task support.

title: Google Cloud donates A2A to Linux Foundation
quote: On 2025-06-23 Google said A2A was transferred to a Linux Foundation-hosted project with neutral governance and community stewardship.

title: Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol Specification
quote: The latest released A2A specification is 1.0.0 and defines discovery, modality negotiation, task management, and secure information exchange between independent agents.

title: Agent Discovery
quote: A2A discovery supports well-known Agent Cards at /.well-known/agent-card.json, curated registries, and direct configuration patterns.

title: Enterprise Implementation of A2A
quote: Production A2A should run over HTTPS, advertise auth in the Agent Card, use HTTP-layer credentials, and integrate with tracing, auditing, and API management.

title: A2A and MCP
quote: A2A and MCP are complementary: A2A is for stateful inter-agent collaboration, while MCP is more about agents using capabilities.

title: Extensions in A2A
quote: Extensions can add data, requirements, RPC methods, and state machines, but they should not fragment or replace the core protocol.

title: Agent2Agent (A2A) Project
quote: The official project lists the core A2A spec plus SDKs for Python, JS/TS, Java, Go, .NET, and Rust, along with samples, inspector, and TCK.

title: A2A Python SDK
quote: The official Python SDK supports FastAPI or Starlette, gRPC, OpenTelemetry, and SQL backends; its public README still highlights spec 0.3 with a separate 1.0-dev track.

title: A2A JavaScript SDK
quote: The official JavaScript SDK shows an Express-based server using AgentExecutor, DefaultRequestHandler, InMemoryTaskStore, and JSON-RPC, REST, and gRPC transports.

title: Agent Skills & Agent Card
quote: An Agent Skill declares id, name, description, tags, examples, media types, and security requirements; the Agent Card exposes those capabilities for discovery.

title: The Agent Executor
quote: The AgentExecutor is the core request-processing abstraction and emits Task, Message, TaskStatusUpdateEvent, and TaskArtifactUpdateEvent objects through an event queue.

title: a2a.server.request_handlers.default_request_handler module
quote: DefaultRequestHandler coordinates the AgentExecutor, TaskStore, QueueManager, and optional push notifier across the standard A2A methods.

title: a2a.server.request_handlers.rest_handler module
quote: The REST handler maps REST-like JSON+HTTP requests to the request handler methods and keeps them equivalent to the gRPC service definitions.
